raw_response: {"response":"Frame the next response around refund triage for a support lead. The activity is reviewing one refund case, interpreting the available customer, order, payment, policy, and evidence signals, then choosing whether to approve, route to policy review, or return for missing evidence. Keep record structure and diagnostic machinery out of the primary surface; translate source details into decision evidence, policy reasons, case history, and follow-up requirements.","next_action":"Write the next agent-facing plan as an operator review workflow: identify the decision the support lead must make, list the evidence needed to make it, define the three outcomes and their required reason capture, and specify what changes after each outcome. Include a small clarification step only for details that determine visibility, policy routing, or missing-evidence handling.","questions":["Which case details should the support lead rely on as visible decision evidence, and which should remain audit-only or diagnostic?","What policy conditions require routing to policy review instead of direct approval?"],"handoff":"Handoff: Owner: next UI agent; Action: convert the refund case source into a support-lead review workflow with approve, policy-review, and missing-evidence outcomes; Reason: the screen must support refund decisioning rather than expose record structure; Evidence gap: confirm visible evidence versus audit-only details and verify every outcome captures a reason and next route.","rationale":"The brief already establishes the user, activity, and primary decisions, so the response should proceed with a concrete domain workflow. The only unresolved context is the mapping from source details to user-facing evidence and the policy boundary between approval and review."}

parsed.response: Frame the next response around refund triage for a support lead. The activity is reviewing one refund case, interpreting the available customer, order, payment, policy, and evidence signals, then choosing whether to approve, route to policy review, or return for missing evidence. Keep record structure and diagnostic machinery out of the primary surface; translate source details into decision evidence, policy reasons, case history, and follow-up requirements.
parsed.next_action: Write the next agent-facing plan as an operator review workflow: identify the decision the support lead must make, list the evidence needed to make it, define the three outcomes and their required reason capture, and specify what changes after each outcome. Include a small clarification step only for details that determine visibility, policy routing, or missing-evidence handling.
questions: Which case details should the support lead rely on as visible decision evidence, and which should remain audit-only or diagnostic?
What policy conditions require routing to policy review instead of direct approval?
parsed.handoff: Handoff: Owner: next UI agent; Action: convert the refund case source into a support-lead review workflow with approve, policy-review, and missing-evidence outcomes; Reason: the screen must support refund decisioning rather than expose record structure; Evidence gap: confirm visible evidence versus audit-only details and verify every outcome captures a reason and next route.
parsed.rationale: The brief already establishes the user, activity, and primary decisions, so the response should proceed with a concrete domain workflow. The only unresolved context is the mapping from source details to user-facing evidence and the policy boundary between approval and review.

execution.command_display: codex exec --model gpt-5.5 -c model_reasoning_effort="xhigh" --sandbox read-only --skip-git-repo-check --ephemeral --color never --output-schema <schema> --output-last-message <output> -
